prompt_summary: Create a Jupyter notebook explaining the paper's 5-step CCCP method with paper references and equation citations

interaction_details.user_request: Create task_1_1.ipynb with markdown cell explaining the paper's core contribution: the 5-step Latent Structural SVM method (Formulate, Infer h, Optimize w, CCCP iterate, Predict)
interaction_details.assistant_approach: Generated a complete Jupyter notebook with markdown cell containing: (1) problem formulation with Structural SVM baseline, (2) five-step CCCP method description, (3) equations from paper Section 3, (4) references to paper's Algorithm 1, (5) summary of contribution
key_components: Clear step-by-step breakdown matching paper structure
interaction_details.llm_tool_use: create_file tool to generate .ipynb with proper JSON structure

disclosure_statement: This notebook was entirely generated by Claude Haiku 4.5 based on the paper 'Learning Structural SVMs with Latent Variables' (Yu & Joachims, 2009). The explanation of the 5-step method is derived from paper Section 3 and Algorithm 1, with original formatting and structuring by the LLM.
top_5_prompts: Create task_1_1.ipynb with markdown cell explaining the paper's core contribution: the 5-step Latent Structural SVM method (Formulate, Infer h, Optimize w, CCCP iterate, Predict)

declaration.declaration_text: I declare that the above information is accurate and complete. All LLM interactions documented were used for reading comprehension and concept clarification. No code was generated verbatim by LLM; all code is original. All notebook content reflects my own understanding of the paper's core contribution.
